strengths: Highest precision for current roster membership
limitations: Can be stale until annual update
May mix trainees and leadership on some pages
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept person-program membership when the page is official and the section label is trainee-facing.

strengths: Strong identity anchor when linked from roster
limitations: Can include contact details that should be redacted
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept if linked from an official roster or exact person/program/institution match on an official Penn/CHOP page.

strengths: Profile URL is anchored by an official trainee roster
Often exposes richer education, prior-training, research, career-interest, and personal context
Can retain display-safety metadata for personal fields
limitations: Coverage is uneven by department and program
Personal fields should not be default-display or outreach signals without explicit product policy
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept only the roster-linked official profile URL as a profile-location fact. Keep extracted education, prior-training, interest, and personal-context fields as candidate enrichment until independently reconciled or explicitly reviewed.

strengths: Queue-driven coverage of medical-school and prior-residency enrichment gaps
Can find long-tail biosketches, CVs, alumni pages, newsletters, and institutional profiles
Stores query, source, candidate, and required-next-evidence provenance
limitations: Education and prior-training pages can be stale
Name collisions are common without profile, program, date, or institutional anchors
utilities.acceptance_rule: Use for discovery only. Promote education or prior-training facts only when the page supplies same-person context and an independent anchor, or after explicit reviewer acceptance.

strengths: Connects authors, works, institutions, topics, ORCID/ROR when available
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept only with at least two identity anchors beyond name, such as Penn affiliation, ORCID, profile link, coauthor cluster, specialty/topic, or prior institution.

limitations: Name-only result counts can be misleading
utilities.acceptance_rule: Author-query counts are discovery evidence only. Article-level candidates can advance to review when the target author, affiliation, topic, or prior-institution anchors agree; accept only after identity review confirms at least two non-name anchors.

strengths: Checks target author against article author XML
Can inspect affiliation, publication year, journal, and topic evidence
limitations: Affiliations may be missing or attached only to first/corresponding authors
Prior-institution and topic matches are suggestive, not identity proof
utilities.acceptance_rule: Promote to review when article author matches and at least one non-name anchor appears. Accept only after manual or automated reconciliation finds two independent non-name anchors.

strengths: Strong when directly linked from profile/publication
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept when ORCID is linked from an official profile/publication or multiple public fields match the trainee.
Accept only with strong name, location, specialty, and institutional agreement; never by name alone.

strengths: Useful for accreditation and lifecycle grounding
limitations: Local tracks and facility-specific rows can share broad specialty labels
Non-ACGME programs legitimately lack registry codes
utilities.acceptance_rule: Use as a candidate program identifier only after local program, specialty, institution, city, and track/facility context agree.

strengths: Useful for attendings and research-intensive fellows
utilities.acceptance_rule: Accept only when investigator identity is anchored by institution, specialty, profile, or publication network.

strengths: Finds long-tail profiles, PDFs, conference abstracts, alumni pages
limitations: May expose stale or personal pages
utilities.acceptance_rule: Use for discovery; promote only after the target page itself passes source and identity checks.
